scope: mythic, monstrous, disaster-scale, deep-sea, sky, divine, or cosmic subjects whose scale and pressure must be witnessed inside a scene

good_means: human-scale or instrument-scale witness makes enormity legible
human, environment, and colossal scales form one readable ladder
one partial subject fragment creates decisive pressure without erasing the scene
the large force produces physically credible environment feedback
partial visibility and frame overflow preserve mystery and weight
bad_means: full centered poster display replaces witnessed presence
pressure is simulated with unrelated lightning, debris, or bright effects
environment width disappears merely to make the subject larger
veto_constraints: must not freeze without a witness or instrument scale anchor
must not freeze without physical environment feedback

gain_moves: increase partial visibility while preserving scene width

operating_intensity.2: wide environment and quieter witnessed scale
operating_intensity.3: balanced scene width, director intention, and pressure
operating_intensity.4: one decisive partial threat with preserved witness field
operating_intensity.5: maximum one-frame oppression with explicit narrow-field risk

guardrails: make destruction consequence rather than decoration
do not apply this adapter to unrelated quiet or intimate scenes
